name: We Bowling?!
short_description: WeBowling?! is built from the ground up for Speedrunners. In this game you take control of a bowling ball of your choosing and make your way through the obstacles as fast as you can! With 10 current tracks and global steam leaderboards, there are plenty of opportunities for you to get that WR!
detailed_description: "We Bowling?!" is what happens when a bowling ball escapes the alley and goes on a wild adventure! Roll, bounce, and yeet your way through obstacle courses packed with ramps, seesaws, moving platforms, and even minigolf-style chaos. The pins are waiting at the end, but can you get there fast enough to top the leaderboards? Speed, precision, and a little bit of luck will decide if you’re a strike or a total gutterball. So, We Bowling!? Or are you gonna split?
about_the_game: "We Bowling?!" is what happens when a bowling ball escapes the alley and goes on a wild adventure! Roll, bounce, and yeet your way through obstacle courses packed with ramps, seesaws, moving platforms, and even minigolf-style chaos. The pins are waiting at the end, but can you get there fast enough to top the leaderboards? Speed, precision, and a little bit of luck will decide if you’re a strike or a total gutterball. So, We Bowling!? Or are you gonna split?

reviews.review_text:
We Bowling?! is a simple, quirky, and surprisingly entertaining little game that turns the classic concept of bowling into a physics-based obstacle course. Instead of lining up a throw, you are the bowling ball, navigating each lane using WASD to reach the pins and (hopefully) score a strike.

The premise is intentionally minimalistic, but that’s exactly what makes the experience so fun. Each lane acts as its own miniature puzzle, with shifting layouts, moving hazards, wavy platforms, and unpredictable geometry that add more challenge than you'd expect from something that looks so straightforward. Some stages require precision, others require timing, and some will YEET you into oblivion if you’re not careful—part of the charm.

The game doesn’t overstay its welcome. In 2–3 hours, you’ll clear dozens of levels that steadily ramp in difficulty while maintaining that lighthearted, "just one more try" loop. It’s extremely easy to pick up, thanks to its simple controls, and works perfectly as a short break game or a fun distraction between bigger titles.

For a free game, it offers plenty of creativity, a good variety of levels, and a genuinely amusing gameplay twist on a familiar sport. Whether you breeze through or get repeatedly knocked off the map, the ride remains enjoyable from start to finish.

— The Ember Archives
https://store.steampowered.com/curator/45601239/
